---
title: ElevenLabs integration rules
scope: project
---

<!-- Generated by `harness rules bootstrap`. Edit to tune; re-run to regenerate. -->

- NEVER expose `xi-api-key` to the browser: no `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` var, v0/React client component, Playwright page context, or committed config. Call ElevenLabs only from Next.js API routes / the GitHub Actions worker. For any client-side audio, mint a server-side **single-use token** instead.
- MUST read the key at call time from env (`process.env.ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` / `os.environ["ELEVENLABS_API_KEY"]`) and fail fast with a clear error if missing. Use a **scope-restricted (TTS-only) key with a credit quota**; keep separate low-quota keys for dev/CI vs prod (there is no sandbox base URL).
- MUST treat `voice_id`, `model_id`, and `output_format` as config, not hardcoded constants; confirm current model IDs against the live API reference rather than pasting a possibly-retired ID. Prefer the official SDKs (`elevenlabs`, `@elevenlabs/elevenlabs-js`) over hand-rolled HTTP.
- TTS has **no idempotency-key header** — enforce it yourself: compute a deterministic content hash of `(approved text + voice_id + model_id + output_format)`, and if S3 already holds that object, reuse it and skip the call. Make `generate_narration` safe to re-enter (LangGraph nodes re-run on retry/resume).
- MUST bound concurrent TTS calls below the subscription tier's concurrency cap (Free 2 / Starter 3 / Creator 5 / Pro 10 / Scale 15). NEVER fan out one narration call per feature/branch across LangGraph nodes unguarded — use a semaphore/queue sized to the tier.
- On **429, branch on the error `code`** — do not blanket-retry: `rate_limit_exceeded` → exponential backoff + jitter (cap ~32s); `concurrent_limit_exceeded` → queue/throttle (backoff won't help); `system_busy` → plain retry. NEVER retry 4xx validation errors (bad voice/model/params) — fix the input. Always log `request_id`.
- For ffmpeg assembly, materialize the audio fully to disk/S3 before `assemble_video_ffmpeg`; a partial streamed response yields truncated/corrupt audio.
- (Webhooks) MUST verify the `ElevenLabs-Signature` header (`t=<ts>,v0=<hmac>`, HMAC-SHA256 over `timestamp + "." + raw_body`) against the **raw request body** before processing — disable Next.js body parsing / use `await req.text()`. NEVER verify against re-serialized parsed JSON.
- (Webhooks) Use the SDK helper (`construct_event(rawBody, sig_header, secret)` / `constructEvent(payload, signature, secret)`) — it checks the signature and enforces the **~30-min timestamp tolerance** (replay protection). Mind the differing arg names/order between Python and JS. Return 401 on bad signature, 200 quickly on success.
- (Webhooks) Handler MUST be idempotent — webhooks are redelivered; dedup on event/`request_id` before acting. Ensure host clock is NTP-synced so skew doesn't fail the 30-min tolerance.
- In CI/Playwright, stub ElevenLabs by default (assert request shape) and gate real calls behind an explicit flag, so demo-capture stays deterministic and credit-free.
